reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add a mount option changes the filesystem configuration. An error could prevent /dev/shm from mounting correctly, leading to boot issues or service failures. According to policy, filesystem/partition changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if /etc/fstab is misconfigured; Disruption of services that rely on /dev/shm; Requires system reboot to apply changes; Misconfiguration could render the system unbootable
